programs.name: MassHealth Medicare Savings Program (Buy-In)
programs.what_it_does: Pays Medicare Part A/B premiums, deductibles, copays for low-income seniors (covers QMB, SLMB, QI equivalents). Ensures affordable access to Medicare-covered services.[7]
programs.who_its_for: Seniors 65+ on Medicare with income ≤135% FPL (QMB), ≤120% (SLMB), assets ≤$2,000 individual.[7]
programs.benefit_value: Saves $2,000-$5,000/year in premiums/cost-sharing depending on level (full for QMB).[7]

programs.name: Frail Elder Waiver (FEW)
programs.what_it_does: Provides home and community-based services as an alternative to nursing homes for frail elders. Includes personal care, homemaker services, home modifications, meal delivery, and emergency response systems.[1]
programs.who_its_for: Nursing home-qualified seniors 65+ with income ≤$2,829/month and assets ≤$2,000, including those with Alzheimer's/dementia.[1][2]
programs.benefit_value: Waiver services valued equivalent to institutional care; no copays for most, covers costs up to nursing home rates.[1]

programs.name: Program of All-Inclusive Care for the Elderly (PACE)
programs.what_it_does: Integrates Medicare and Medicaid benefits into one comprehensive program for long-term care. Offers medical care, LTC, dental, eye care, and adult day health to enable community living.[1]
programs.who_its_for: Dual-eligible (Medicare + MassHealth) seniors 55+ certified as nursing facility level of care, living in PACE service area.[1]
programs.benefit_value: All-inclusive capitation covers unlimited LTC, extras like dental; no/low premiums/copays.[1][6]

programs.name: SNAP (Food Stamps)
programs.what_it_does: Provides monthly EBT card for groceries to low-income seniors. Administered by MA DTA with senior-specific deductions.[4]
programs.who_its_for: Seniors 60+ with income ≤200% FPL (~$30,000/year couple); higher asset limits.[4]
programs.benefit_value: Average $200-$300/month per senior household on EBT.[4]

programs.name: Fuel Assistance (LIHEAP)
programs.what_it_does: Helps pay heating costs during winter for low-income households. Provides direct payments to fuel vendors.[4]
programs.who_its_for: Seniors 60+ with income ≤60% state median (~$40,000/year household).[4]
programs.benefit_value: $200-$1,500 per heating season based on household size/income.[4]

programs.name: Residential Conservation Service (Weatherization)
programs.what_it_does: Free home energy efficiency upgrades like insulation, sealing, furnace tune-ups via DOE/LEAP programs. Reduces energy bills long-term.[4]
programs.who_its_for: Low-income seniors 60+ (≤200% FPL) or via MassHealth referral.[4]
programs.benefit_value: $4,000-$8,000 in free upgrades per home; 20-30% energy savings.[4]

programs.name: SHINE (Serving Health Information Needs of Elders)
programs.what_it_does: Free Medicare counseling and enrollment assistance by trained volunteers. Helps compare plans, appeal denials.[4]
programs.who_its_for: All MA Medicare beneficiaries 65+, caregivers, no income limits.[4]
programs.benefit_value: Saves avg $1,500/year via optimal plan selection; unlimited phone/in-person help.[4]

programs.name: Meals on Wheels
programs.what_it_does: Delivers nutritious hot meals to homebound seniors 5-7 days/week. Includes safety check-ins by deliverers.[4]
programs.who_its_for: Homebound 60+ unable to prepare meals; free/low-cost based on income.[4]
programs.benefit_value: 1-2 meals/day; serves 70,000+ MA seniors, free for most qualifying.[4]

programs.name: Caregiver Respite Program
programs.what_it_does: Funds temporary relief for family caregivers via PCA or adult day. Prevents caregiver burnout.[4]
programs.who_its_for: Family caregivers of 60+ frail elders via Councils on Aging/Area Agencies.[4]
programs.benefit_value: Up to $1,600/year vouchers for respite services.[4]

programs.name: SCSEP (Senior Community Service Employment Program)
programs.what_it_does: Part-time paid community service jobs with training for employment. Minimum wage, 20 hrs/week.[4]
programs.who_its_for: Unemployed 55+ with income ≤125% FPL seeking work.[4]

programs.name: Massachusetts Senior Legal Helpline
programs.what_it_does: Free legal advice hotlines for housing, benefits, abuse issues. Referrals to pro bono attorneys.[4]
programs.who_its_for: Low-income 60+ MA residents; priority for benefits/consumer issues.[4]
programs.benefit_value: Unlimited advice calls; recovers $millions in benefits annually.[4]

programs.name: Long-Term Care Ombudsman Program
programs.what_it_does: Advocates for residents in nursing homes/assisted living resolving complaints. Protects rights confidentially.[4]
programs.who_its_for: All LTC facility residents/families in MA; no eligibility requirements.[4]
programs.benefit_value: Resolves 85% complaints without litigation; statewide 24/7 access.[4]

programs.name: Prescription Advantage
programs.what_it_does: Provides prescription drug coverage to eligible Massachusetts residents. It helps cover costs for medications not fully paid by Medicare or other insurance.
programs.who_its_for: Massachusetts residents age 65 and older, or younger individuals with disabilities who meet income and employment guidelines.
programs.whats_unique: State-funded pharmaceutical assistance program specific to Massachusetts, supplementing federal Medicare coverage for low-income seniors.
programs.benefit_value: Varies by income and drug costs; covers copays and premiums beyond federal programs.

programs.name: Home Care Program (HCP)
programs.what_it_does: Delivers in-home care management, personal care, homemaker services, and support to enable aging in place. Services include transportation, meals, laundry, grocery shopping, and companionship through Aging Services Access Points (ASAPs).
programs.who_its_for: Massachusetts seniors age 60+, people with disabilities, and those with early-stage dementia able to complete some activities of daily living (ADLs).
programs.whats_unique: State-administered through 27 nonprofit ASAPs with comprehensive interdisciplinary assessments; uses sliding scale copays, distinct from federal HCBS waivers.
programs.benefit_value: Customized care plans; services valued at thousands annually based on needs, with copays on sliding scale.

programs.name: Enhanced Community Options Program (ECOP)
programs.what_it_does: Provides intensive in-home care and non-medical assistance for those needing higher levels of support to avoid nursing home placement. Includes care planning by social workers and family caregiver support.
programs.who_its_for: Massachusetts seniors requiring greater degree of care than standard home care, often at risk of institutionalization.
programs.whats_unique: Massachusetts-specific program reserved for higher-needs elders; complements HCP with more intensive services and sliding scale fees.
programs.benefit_value: Expanded in-home services preventing nursing home costs (avg. $100K+/year); copays on sliding scale.

programs.name: Home Modification and Loan Program
programs.what_it_does: Offers low-interest loans for home adaptations to support aging in place. Covers ramps, stair lifts, kitchen/bathroom modifications, and security features.
programs.who_its_for: Massachusetts homeowners, primarily seniors and people with disabilities needing accessibility improvements.
programs.whats_unique: State-specific 0%-3% interest loans up to $30,000 administered by Massachusetts Rehabilitation Commission, beyond federal options.
programs.benefit_value: Loans up to $30,000 at 0%-3% interest, enabling modifications costing $5,000-$25,000+.

programs.name: Commonwealth Coordinated Care (CCC)
programs.what_it_does: Integrates health, long-term care, and community social services through participating health plans. Coordinates care to support aging in place.
programs.who_its_for: Massachusetts seniors age 65+ eligible for MassHealth (Medicaid).
programs.whats_unique: Massachusetts state program linking MassHealth with elder services via Department of Senior Services, beyond standard Medicaid managed care.
programs.benefit_value: Comprehensive coordinated services valued at $20,000+ annually in long-term care support.
